meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Finish AI Projects (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your AI projects. Learn a professional client-centric workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your portfolio apps faster.
content.answer_block: You fail to finish projects because you code in a vacuum without constraints. By adopting a 'Client-First' workflow, you treat your personal projects as professional contracts. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution to force project completion through structured milestones and real-world delivery constraints.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions. You start with a burst of inspiration, only to drown in scope creep and architectural paralysis by week three. The difference between a student who plays with code and an AI Engineer who ships products is the workflow that governs their development cycle.

To move from 'I'm learning' to 'I've built', you must externalize your constraints. By treating your own project as a high-stakes client engagement, you replace aimless hacking with disciplined execution. This checklist provides the exact scaffolding, using Antigravity and Claude Code, to transform your next prototype into a production-ready asset.

steps.title: Define the 'Client' Scope
steps.description: Establish a rigid Project Requirements Document (PRD) as if you are accountable to a paying client. This prevents feature bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea and generate a 3-phase PRD. Define the MVP, secondary features, and out-of-scope items to ensure a 2-week delivery timeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a PRD.md file outlining the MVP features and technical constraints for this project.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Visualize your state management and API interactions before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mermaid diagram for the data flow of this application, specifically focusing on the integration between the frontend and the AI backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a system_architecture.md file based on the PRD, focusing on efficient data flow between the LLM and the database.'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Scaffolding
steps.description: Use automated scaffolding to generate boilerplate, allowing you to focus on unique value rather than setup.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project structure for a Next.js app with Supabase integration and an AI-agent endpoint. Follow clean architecture principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project boilerplate using the generated architecture requirements.'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation Cycles
steps.description: Break development into 4-hour sprints. Use Claude Code to execute specific modules to maintain momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break the MVP into 4-hour implementable tasks. Prioritize the core AI feedback loop.
steps.claude_code_command: claude task 'Implement the core auth middleware and database schema as defined in the PRD.'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Before marking a feature 'done', ensure it passes automated checks to prevent technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the core AI integration logic. Ensure edge cases like token limits and API failures are handled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute test suite and fix any regressions found in the previous sprint.'

pro_tips: Treat your README.md as a sales page for your project; if you can't describe the value in three sentences, the project is too complex.
Use Claude Code to audit your own code for 'Student Syndrome'—look for over-engineering where a simple library would suffice.
Always build the 'happy path' first; never optimize for edge cases until the core user journey works end-to-end.
Implement a 'Feature Freeze' date in your calendar. If it's not finished by then, ship the MVP as-is.
Document your failures in a 'Technical Debt' file; it shows future employers you understand how to manage trade-offs.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel or Railway, tag your repository with 'AI-Engineered', and start your next project with a 30% tighter timeline.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the workflow to stop starting and start shipping. Stop letting your potential die in a private repo. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
